title: West Coast June
meta_desc: Discover the West Coast in June with kids, enjoy mild weather, fewer crowds and exciting family activities from $150-$250/day for a family of 4
intro: June is a great time to visit the West Coast with kids, with mild winter weather, fewer crowds and exciting family activities. The region offers a range of free and low-cost activities, including the famous Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and stunning glacier walks. With prices starting from $150-$250/day for a family of 4, it's an affordable winter holiday option.

sections.heading: Weather in June
sections.body:
June is winter in New Zealand, but the West Coast's mild oceanic climate means average highs of 12°C and lows of 6°C. It's a great time to enjoy the region's stunning natural beauty without the crowds.

While it can be rainy, the scenery is particularly lush and green, making it a great time for photography and nature walks.

Make sure to pack warm and waterproof clothing, including jackets, gloves and hats, to keep your family cozy and dry.

sections.heading: What's On in June
sections.body:
June is a quiet month on the West Coast, but there are still some exciting events and activities to enjoy.

The Hokitika Winter Festival usually takes place in June, featuring live music, food stalls and craft markets.

The West Coast Wilderness Trail is also a great option for families, with guided bike tours and stunning scenery.

sections.heading: Best Activities for Families
sections.body:
The West Coast offers a range of exciting activities for families, from stunning natural beauty to adventurous outdoor pursuits.

The Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and Blowholes are a must-visit, with a short walk and stunning ocean views.

The Franz Josef and Fox Glaciers are also accessible by short walks, with breathtaking scenery and interesting geological formations.
list: Franz Josef Glacier valley walk (free, 3km return)

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
Here are some practical tips for families visiting the West Coast in June:

Book your accommodation and activities ahead of time to avoid crowds and availability issues.

Pack warm and waterproof clothing, including jackets, gloves and hats.

Don't forget your camera and charger, with stunning scenery and photo opportunities around every corner.
list: Book ahead for accommodation and activities
Check the weather forecast before heading out
Bring snacks and water for outdoor activities

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Budget
sections.body:
June is a quiet month on the West Coast, with fewer crowds and a more relaxed atmosphere.

Prices for accommodation and activities are also lower, with an average daily budget of $150-$250 for a family of 4.

Make sure to book ahead for popular activities and accommodation, but also be flexible and spontaneous to make the most of your trip.
